jokes.text: I think surprise parties are tacky, and far too often organized by well-intentioned people with very bad taste. But... Patrick has always wanted one, poor thing.
jokes.setup: David calls surprise parties tacky while organizing one
jokes.punchline: The condescending 'poor thing' reveals his superiority complex
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment - his snobby contradictions while being loving underneath

jokes.text: When I planned that Casablanca-themed party for your mother's 40th, I had to quarantine the camels for a month.
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions planning elaborate parties
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail about quarantining camels
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail that reveals the family's former wealth in an unexpected way

jokes.text: I've actually been organizing this for quite some time, I'm just telling you right now because I know that none of you can keep a secret.
jokes.setup: Family questions his last-minute planning
jokes.punchline: David admits to not trusting his family with secrets
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing David's controlling nature and family dynamics

jokes.text: Oh! At 9 a.m. it's a little early for character assassination.
jokes.setup: David criticizes the family's secret-keeping
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic overstatement about 'character assassination'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira dramatic overreaction with perfect vocabulary choice

jokes.text: Not one of you knows how the 'Crows' movie ends. You told me the day you got home. You gave me the script.
jokes.setup: David proves his point about family keeping secrets
jokes.punchline: The escalating reveals of how badly they spoiled the movie
jokes.explanation: Good escalating comedy showing family dysfunction through spoilers

jokes.text: Yes, things are certainly racing along at a dangerously rapid velocity.
jokes.setup: Parents comment on meeting Patrick's parents being a big step
jokes.punchline: David's dramatically anxious response to normal relationship progression
jokes.explanation: Perfect David anxiety about relationships moving too fast

jokes.text: How did it end again? Like, a crowmageddon? Crowpocalypse.
jokes.setup: Previous discussion about spoiling the Crows movie
jokes.punchline: The absurd portmanteau words for the movie ending
jokes.explanation: Fun wordplay that callbacks to the spoiler theme effectively

jokes.text: I asked you to mow the lawn three times, Roland, so you being here is more your decision than mine.
jokes.setup: Roland acts like he's invited to share this special moment
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dry correction that Roland is avoiding work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's exasperation with Roland

jokes.text: Oh, no, no! I do get that all the time, though. It's uh, you know, you've got David's handsome looks there, and then my face.
jokes.setup: Patrick's parents assume Roland is David's dad
jokes.punchline: Roland's backhanded self-deprecating explanation
jokes.explanation: Standard self-deprecating humor that fits Roland's character

jokes.text: It's uh, you know, you've got David's handsome looks there, and then my face.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining the supposed resemblance
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment that combines their features
jokes.explanation: Roland's awkward attempt to compliment himself while complimenting David. Moderately funny character work.

jokes.text: More like, runs it into the ground.
jokes.setup: Patrick's dad mentions Johnny runs the motel
jokes.punchline: Roland's immediate undermining comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland undermining Johnny in front of guests

jokes.text: Oh, we do have a lot of fun around here, don't we? We do, sometimes.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to smooth over Roland's insult
jokes.punchline: His own weak attempt at positivity that admits it's only sometimes fun
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Johnny's struggle to be diplomatic

jokes.text: But uh, I'll have to admit, I've been somewhat of a skeptic about those two. This one isn't as progressive as the rest of us. It's a generational thing with him. I was talking about the store, Roland.
jokes.setup: Clint seems to express concern about the relationship
jokes.punchline: The reveal he meant the business, not their sexuality
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts expectations about homophobia

jokes.text: Really? Because it kind of seems like the two of you just found out some really shocking information.
jokes.setup: Patrick's parents react awkwardly to learning about the relationship
jokes.punchline: Roland bluntly calling out their obvious shock
jokes.explanation: Roland's lack of social awareness creates uncomfortable truth-telling

jokes.text: Correct me if I'm wrong here, Johnny. But it kinda seems like they didn't know their son is in a relationship with David.
jokes.setup: The awkward revelation scene
jokes.punchline: Roland stating the obvious uncomfortable truth
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Roland being obliviously direct

jokes.text: Well, according to the radio, there's a darling little medical clinic in Elm Grove. Where if any one of us, say, you, or your father, participates in a few nugatory tests, we could be bringing in over $200 a week!
jokes.setup: Moira mentions finding revenue streams
jokes.punchline: Her elaborate way of suggesting medical testing for money
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira verbose delivery and the word 'nugatory' is perfect

jokes.text: Showing up, taking your picture with a motley crew of pale, dewy-faced salamander people.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why she avoids fan conventions
jokes.punchline: Her elaborate, cruel description of fans as 'salamander people'
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - the specific, cruel imagery is perfectly in character and hilarious

jokes.text: You did a commercial for adult diapers! In Japan! In full-face Kabuki makeup!
jokes.setup: Moira claims she hasn't hit rock bottom
jokes.punchline: Alexis's escalating list of embarrassing career choices
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with specific absurd details about Moira's career

jokes.text: But there will be a safe word in case the gaggle of asymmetrical faces gets to be too much.
jokes.setup: Moira agrees to do the convention
jokes.punchline: Her cruel description of fans as 'asymmetrical faces'
jokes.explanation: Another perfectly cruel Moira description - 'asymmetrical faces' is wonderfully specific

jokes.text: Okay so I told Patrick that there's a salmonella outbreak at the café, so he will not be coming here until I tell him to tonight. Is it possible you could maybe choose a lie that doesn't make the café look like it's in violation of health codes?
jokes.setup: David explains his plan to keep Patrick away
jokes.punchline: Stevie pointing out the obvious flaw in his lie
jokes.explanation: Good practical comedy showing David's lack of foresight

jokes.text: Did you run here, Mr. Rose? No! Just a pleasant walk.
jokes.setup: Johnny arrives clearly out of breath/rushed
jokes.punchline: His defensive denial of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with Johnny's obvious lie

jokes.text: romantically in business with each other. Ew!
jokes.setup: Johnny tries to explain the relationship awkwardly
jokes.punchline: Stevie's disgusted reaction to the phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Johnny's awkward phrasing

jokes.text: I mean, what do they think, I'm just his business partner?! Possibly. I mean, yeah.
jokes.setup: David is outraged at the suggestion
jokes.punchline: Johnny and Stevie confirming that's exactly what they think
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of David's indignation with matter-of-fact responses

jokes.text: Well, that sounds reasonable. What did I just say?!
jokes.setup: David tells everyone to calm down and not say anything
jokes.punchline: Johnny immediately contradicting him, then David's exasperation
jokes.explanation: Standard family miscommunication comedy

jokes.text: You took me to the Playboy Mansion when I was seven. And you had a wonderful time in the children's grotto.
jokes.setup: Moira claims she's protected Alexis from the industry's dark side
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casual mention of inappropriate childhood experiences
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about inappropriate parenting delivered casually

jokes.text: She only played Grace on 'Touched With Grace' for 37 years. The woman has 17 Emmys for playing a hot-headed nurse who healed people with her sexual touch.
jokes.setup: Moira explains who Gloria Gregson is
jokes.punchline: The absurd concept of healing through sexual touch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of soap opera concepts taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Oh! Daughter? More like sister. Ew.
jokes.setup: Gloria makes inappropriate comment about Alexis looking like Moira's sister
jokes.punchline: Alexis's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe comedy from inappropriate compliment

jokes.text: Flowers are for you; the pizza's my lunch.
jokes.setup: Stevie arrives at Patrick's with flowers and pizza
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact clarification about the pizza
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery and practical nature

jokes.text: Yeah, I talked to my parents a little while ago. They're on like, a weekend getaway, so they couldn't chat long
jokes.setup: David asking if Patrick talked to his parents
jokes.punchline: Patrick lying about where his parents are when they're literally at the motel
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony as audience knows the truth Patrick is avoiding

jokes.text: Um... [long pause] Okay listen, David...
jokes.setup: David asking if Patrick's parents know about them
jokes.punchline: Patrick's long uncomfortable pause before having to confess
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of silence to create comedy through discomfort and revelation

jokes.text: Okay.
jokes.setup: Patrick reveals he hasn't told his parents about their relationship
jokes.punchline: David's stunned, single-word response
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - the silence says everything about David's shock

jokes.text: That's why I brought this couple home one day in college, and just told my parents to deal with it.
jokes.setup: David talks about coming out on your own terms
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of bringing home 'a couple' in college
jokes.explanation: Great character detail about David's past and approach to relationships

jokes.text: You're gonna laugh yourself right out of the closet on this one.
jokes.setup: David is about to reveal the surprise party news
jokes.punchline: The dark irony of the expression given Patrick's actual closet situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark irony - the phrase becomes literally meaningful in context

jokes.text: The salmonella was a decoy.
jokes.setup: Previous mention of salmonella outbreak excuse
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact revelation it was fake
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier lie with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: The salmonella was a decoy.
jokes.setup: Patrick asking if the party is at the café
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact revelation of his elaborate lie
jokes.explanation: Good callback with David treating his deception like a military operation

jokes.text: Oh, no. I very much am.
jokes.setup: Patrick's parents asking if he's not in a relationship with their son
jokes.punchline: David's blunt, matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - direct and unapologetic in the most awkward situation

jokes.text: For a minute I thought this was gonna get very dark.
jokes.setup: David realizes the parents aren't homophobic
jokes.punchline: His casual understatement about potential homophobia
jokes.explanation: Good relief comedy with David's understated reaction to dodging homophobia

jokes.text: keep him in the closet until then. I know that came out wrong, but we all understand what I'm saying, right?
jokes.setup: David suggests not revealing they know about the relationship
jokes.punchline: His awareness of the unfortunate phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good meta-awareness of the accidental double entendre

jokes.text: We used to use that for sangria, because it was like, so cheap, and... so good.
jokes.setup: Gloria mentions her wine brand
jokes.punchline: Alexis's oblivious admission about using it for cheap sangria
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's lack of filter

jokes.text: $20?! I charge $50. And just in case, I wear open-toed sandals.
jokes.setup: Discussion of charging for feet pictures
jokes.punchline: Gloria's professional approach and preparedness
jokes.explanation: Great absurd escalation showing the professional side of convention weirdness

jokes.text: And if we're talking feet, it's $75 for one, $150 for the pair.
jokes.setup: Learning about convention foot picture economics
jokes.punchline: Alexis's immediate professional pricing structure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - immediately adapting to any money-making opportunity

jokes.text: 'Happy birthday' was too wordy, and we couldn't nail it at rehearsal.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they're just saying 'Surprise'
jokes.punchline: The absurd reasoning about 'Happy Birthday' being too complex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Stevie deadpan about the incompetence of the surprise party planning

jokes.text: But might I recommend the crab cakes? I over-ordered, and they cost a small fortune.
jokes.setup: David excusing himself from the family reunion
jokes.punchline: His inability to leave without mentioning his expensive mistake
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's anxiety about money and need to control outcomes

jokes.text: I don't understand his clothes, but... Ah, you will in time.
jokes.setup: Patrick's dad admits he doesn't understand David's fashion
jokes.punchline: David's confident prediction that he'll come around
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's confidence in his fashion sense

jokes.text: I don't understand his clothes, but... Ah, you will in time.
jokes.setup: Patrick's father expressing acceptance
jokes.punchline: His confusion about David's fashion, his wife's confidence he'll adapt
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment that gets humor from the realistic concern about David's unique style
